test_suite.name: Role-Based Access Control Test Suite
test_suite.description: This suite validates authorization rules across AUTHOR, EDITOR, and VIEWER roles, covering ownership-based access, permission-based access, CRUD operations, publish/version flows, engagement features, and privilege escalation prevention.
test_suite.scope: - Article management (draft, update, publish) - Version control - Reaction / engagement features - Viewer read-only access - Role escalation prevention
test_suite.prompt:
Prepare test environment with multiple users having different roles:
- AUTHOR: owns several articles (draft + published) - EDITOR: can manage all articles regardless of ownership - VIEWER: read-only access to published articles
Test data must include: - Articles created by multiple authors - Draft and published articles - Version history for at least one article
Each test case must be executed under the correct authenticated user context using valid tokens/sessions per role.
Validate that: - AUTHOR is restricted to own resources - EDITOR can access and modify all resources within studio scope - VIEWER can only read published content - No unauthorized access returns data - Unauthorized actions return 403 Forbidden - No privilege escalation is possible via API

setup.users: - AUTHOR user (has ownership over multiple articles) - EDITOR user (full permissions within studio) - VIEWER user (read-only)
setup.data_requirements: - Multiple articles from different authors - Mix of draft and published articles - Version history for at least one article
setup.preconditions: - All users must be created in DB - Authentication tokens must be available for each role - Requests must include valid Authorization headers

case.description: AUTHOR can only see own drafts
case.expected_result: Only articles where authorId equals current userId are returned
Returns all drafts without authorId filtering

case.description: VIEWER can only access published articles
Reaction on draft restricted by ownership
case.expected_result: Allowed only if ownership conditions are satisfied

case.description: User cannot escalate role via API
case.steps: Attempt to update role via any endpoint
